title: Monaco explains why agent developer workspaces are hard to build or buy
friendlyTitle: Running many coding agents is harder than it looks
summary: Monaco published a detailed account of its attempt to support many parallel coding agents with isolated environments, realistic test data, and human-in-the-loop workflows. The post compares local tmux and worktree setups with a Coder-based internal workspace approach and notes the tradeoffs against newer vendor platforms.
friendlySummary: Monaco describes what went wrong when it tried to run many AI coding agents at once and how it built better workspaces.
whyItMatters: Many teams want coding agents to run in parallel, but the hard part is safe, repeatable infrastructure: ports, data, disks, dependencies, notifications, and governance. Monaco’s write-up gives engineering leaders a practical map of what breaks before they scale agentic development.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Teams can use these lessons before spending money or time on their own agent coding setup.
